div.p: 6. Step6: Sum over q and Step7: Expression for spin Hall conductivity With use of Eqs.( 29), (30), and ( 27), we obtain We show that the sum of the MT terms with the side jump process, which is shown in Fig. 11, vanishes when we set в„Ұ k = 0. Similar to the DOS terms, the diagram reflected with respect to the horizontal axis contributes equally to the original diagram. The response functions ОҰ xy for each diagram are given by
div.formula: Пғ DOS-SS xy = - e 2 3 О» 2 0 n i v 3 0 N (0) 2 T 2m 2 Оҫ D SC k 2 F D 2 Г— 8ПҖ 2 П„ 4 -3 ПҲ 1 2 + 4ПҖT П„ -ПҲ 1 2 + 4ПҖT П„ ПҲ (1) 1 2 + 4ПҖT П„ + 3ПҲ (1) 1 2 -4ПҖT П„ 2 ПҲ (2) 1 2 Г— пЈұ пЈҙ пЈІ пЈҙ пЈі 1 4ПҖ ln 1 (D = 2) 1 2ПҖ 2 (D = 3).

ОҰ MT-SJ-a xy = - 2T 2 V в„Ұ k Оөn k1k2q ieО» 2 0 8 V (k 1y -k 2y ) - e m (q y -k 1y ) n i v 2 0 V V Г— G(k 1 , iОө n )G(k 1 , iОө n + iПү ОҪ )G(q -k 1 , iв„Ұ k -iОө n )G(q -k 1 , iв„Ұ k -iОө n -iПү ОҪ ) Г— G(k 2 , iОө n ) Г— C(q, Оө n , в„Ұ k -Оө n )C(q, Оө n + Пү ОҪ , в„Ұ k -Оө n -Пү ОҪ )L(q, iв„Ұ k ), ОҰ MT-SJ-b xy = - 2T 2 V в„Ұ k Оөn k1k2q ieО» 2 0 8 V (k 1y -k 2y ) - e m (q y -k 1y ) n i v 2 0 V V Г— G(k 1 , iОө n + iПү ОҪ )G(q -k 1 , iв„Ұ k -iОө n )G(q -k 1 , iв„Ұ k -iОө n -iПү ОҪ ) Г— G(k 2 , iОө n )G(q -k 2 , iв„Ұ k -iОө n ) Г— C(q, Оө n , в„Ұ k -Оө n )C(q, Оө n + Пү ОҪ , в„Ұ k -Оө n -Пү ОҪ )L(q, iв„Ұ k ).

We set в„Ұ k = 0 for all quantities and set q = 0 for Green functions, ОҰ MT-SJ-a xy and ОҰ MT-SJ-b xy reduce to

div.p: with
div.formula: I a = dОҫ 1 G(k 1 , iОө n )G(k 1 , iОө n + iПү ОҪ )G(k 1 , -iОө n )G(k 1 , -iОө n -iПү ОҪ ) dОҫ 2 G(k 2 , iОө n ), I b = dОҫ 1 G(k 1 , iОө n )G(k 1 , -iОө n )G(k 1 , -iОө n -iПү ОҪ ) dОҫ 2 G(k 2 , iОө n )G(k 2 , -iОө n ).

div.head: Step3: Integral over wavevectors
div.p: The Оҫ 1 integrals for I a and I b are performed separately for the three cases of distribution of the poles shown in Fig. 12 or Fig. 13. Performing integral with Оҫ 1 and Оҫ 2 , we obtain
div.formula: I a = -iПҖ 2 sign(Оө n ) 1 | Оө n+ОҪ | + | Оө n | 1 | Оө n+ОҪ || Оө n | , I b = iПҖ 2 sign(Оө n ) 1 | Оө n+ОҪ | + | Оө n | 1 | Оө n+ОҪ || Оө n | ,
div.p: i.e. I a + I b = 0. We thus see that the MT terms with side jump process vanish in total. Figure 14 shows the diagrams for the MT terms the skew scattering process. vertically or horizontally reflected diagrams contribute equally to the original diagram and thus, we focus on the diagrams shown in Fig. 14 and multiply the resultant expressions by four.

We see that (b) is different from (a) only in the arguments of Green functions. In the diagram (c), the vector product part k 2 Г— k 1 is minus that in (a). However, the summation over spin index recovers the same coefficient as (a), including the sign. Thus, we first perform the integral of the product of the Green function with Оҫ 1 and Оҫ 2 for (a), (b), and (c), and then sum them up. The resultant expression for ОҰ MT-SS xy is given by
div.formula: ОҰ MT-SS xy = - 2T 2 V в„Ұ k Оөn k1k2k3q - e 2m k 1x - iО» 2 0 4V (k 1x k 2y -k 1y k 2x ) - e m (q y -k 2y ) 1 V 2 n i v 3 0 V Г— (product of Green functions) Г— C(q, Оө n , в„Ұ k -Оө n )C(q, Оө n + Пү ОҪ , в„Ұ k -Оө n -Пү ОҪ )L(q, iв„Ұ k ) вүҲ -4 reflected diagrams вҖў ie 2 2 О» 2 0 n i v 3 0 4m 2 k 2 F D 2 N (0) 3 вҖў T в„Ұ k q L(q, iв„Ұ k )ОЈ MT-SS (q, в„Ұ k , Пү ОҪ ) вүҲ - ie 2 2 О» 2 0 n i v 3 0 k 4 F N (0) 3 T D 2 m 2 вҖў q L(q, 0)ОЈ MT-SS (q, 0, Пү ОҪ ),
div.p: where we introduce the notations
div.formula: ОЈ MT-SS (q, в„Ұ k , Пү ОҪ ) =T Оөn C(q, Оө n , в„Ұ k -Оө n )C(q, Оө n + Пү ОҪ , в„Ұ k -Оө n -Пү ОҪ )(I a + I b + I c ), I a = dОҫ 1 G(k 1 , iОө n )G(k 1 , iОө n + iПү ОҪ ) Г— dОҫ 2 G(k 2 , iОө n )G(k 2 , iОө n + iПү ОҪ )G(k 2 , -iОө n )G(k 2 , -iОө n -iПү ОҪ ) Г— dОҫ 3 G(k, iОө n ), I b = dОҫ 1 G(k 1 , iОө n )G(k 1 , iОө n + iПү ОҪ ) Г— dОҫ 2 G(k 2 , iОө n + iПү ОҪ )G(k 2 , -iОө n )G(k 2 , -iОө n -iПү ОҪ ) Г— dОҫ 3 G(k 3 , iОө n )G(k 3 , -iОө n ),
I c = dОҫ 1 G(k 1 , iОө n )G(k 1 , iОө n + iПү ОҪ )G(k 1 , -iОө n -iПү ОҪ ) Г— dОҫ 2 G(k 2 , iОө n )G(k 2 , -iОө n )G(k 2 , -iОө n -iПү ОҪ ) Г— dОҫ 3 G(k 3 , iОө n ).

div.head: Step2: Integral over wavevectors
div.p: The Оҫ 2 , Оҫ 3 integrals of I a + I b in the present case are the same as the Оҫ 1 , Оҫ 2 integrals of I a + I b in the MT terms with side jump process and thus it vanishes.

The Оҫ 1 integral and the Оҫ 2 integral in I c are performed, considering the distribution of the poles, which are shown in Fig. 15 and Fig. 16. Performing the integral with Оҫ 1 , Оҫ 2 , Оҫ 3 , we obtain
div.formula: I c = -4iПҖ 3 sign(Оө n+ОҪ ) |2 Оө n ||2 Оө n+ОҪ |(| Оө n+ОҪ | + | Оө n |) 2 .

div.head: With use of it, we calculate ОЈ
div.formula: MT-SS ОЈ MT-SS (q, 0, Пү ОҪ ) =T Оөn C(q, Оө n , -Оө n )C(q, Оө n + Пү ОҪ , -Оө n -Пү ОҪ )I c = -4iПҖ 3 T Оөn |2 Оө n+ОҪ | |2Оө n+ОҪ | + Dq 2 |2 Оө n | |2Оө n | + Dq 2 sign(Оө n+ОҪ ) |2 Оө n ||2 Оө n+ОҪ |(| Оө n+ОҪ | + | Оө n |) 2 = -4iПҖ 3 T Оөn 1 |2Оө n+ОҪ | + Dq 2 1 |2Оө n | + Dq 2 sign(Оө n+ОҪ ) (| Оө n+ОҪ | + | Оө n |) 2 .

div.head: Step4: Sum over Оөn
div.p: We note that the sum over n < -ОҪ and that over n вүҘ 0 cancel with each other; the sum over n < -ОҪ becomes
div.formula: -ОҪ-1 n=-вҲһ 1 |2Оө n+ОҪ | + Dq 2 1 |2Оө n | + Dq 2 sign(Оө n+ОҪ ) (| Оө n+ОҪ | + | Оө n |) 2 = - -ОҪ-1 n=-вҲһ 1 -2Оө n -2Пү ОҪ + Dq 2 1 -2Оө n + Dq 2 1 (-2Оө n -Пү ОҪ + /П„ ) 2 = - -1 n=-вҲһ 1 -2Оө n + Dq 2 1 -2Оө n + 2Пү ОҪ + Dq 2 1 (-2Оө n + Пү ОҪ + /П„ ) 2 = - вҲһ n=0 1 2Оө n + Dq 2 1 2Оө n + 2Пү ОҪ + Dq 2 1 (2Оө n + Пү ОҪ + /П„ ) 2 = - вҲһ n=0 1 |2Оө n | + Dq 2 1 |2Оө n+ОҪ | + Dq 2 1 (| Оө n | + | Оө n+ОҪ |) 2 ,
div.p: which is minus the contribution from n вүҘ 0.
The sum over -ОҪ вүӨ n < 0 becomes
div.formula: ПҖT -1 n=-ОҪ 1 |2Оө n+ОҪ | + Dq 2 1 |2Оө n | + Dq 2 sign(Оө n+ОҪ ) (| Оө n+ОҪ | + | Оө n |) 2 =ПҖT -1 n=-ОҪ 1 2Оө n + 2Пү ОҪ + Dq 2 1 -2Оө n + Dq 2 1 (Оө n + Пү ОҪ + /2П„ -(Оө n -/2П„ )) 2 = ПҖT (Пү ОҪ + /П„ ) 2 -1 n=-ОҪ 1 2Оө n + 2Пү ОҪ + Dq 2 1 -2Оө n + Dq 2 = 1 4(Пү ОҪ + /П„ ) 2 1 Dq 2 + Пү ОҪ ПҲ 1 2 + 2Пү ОҪ 4ПҖT + Dq 2 4ПҖT -ПҲ 1 2 + Dq 2 4ПҖT .

div.p: Next we sum over в„Ұ k , separating the sum into three regions shown in Fig. 19. The function ПҲ (1) (z) is analytic in the region Re(z) > 0 and hence Eq. ( 33) is analytic function of iв„Ұ k in each region. Let B ci , B si (i = 1, 2, 3) be analytic continuation of B s in each region 1 , 2 , and 3 . We note that B s1 (iв„Ұ k , 0) = B s3 (iв„Ұ k , 0) = 0 through explicit calculation
div.formula: B si (iв„Ұ k , 0) = 1 4 ОұОІ 2 m + ОІО» k F Оұ вҖў 1 (8iПҖT )(-2ОұО»k F ) Г— -ПҲ (1) 1 2 Вұ в„Ұ k 4ПҖT + i(-Оұ + ОІ)О»k F 4ПҖT + ПҲ (1) 1 2 Вұ в„Ұ k 4ПҖT - i(-Оұ + ОІ)О»k F 4ПҖT + ПҲ (1) 1 2 Вұ в„Ұ k 4ПҖT + i(Оұ + ОІ)О»k F 4ПҖT -ПҲ 1 2 Вұ в„Ұ k 4ПҖT - i(Оұ + ОІ)О»k F 4ПҖT = 1 4 ОұОІ 2 m + ОІО» k F вҖў 1 (8iПҖT )(-2О»k F ) Г— -ПҲ (1) 1 2 Вұ в„Ұ k 4ПҖT + i(Оұ + ОІ)О»k F 4ПҖT + ПҲ (1) 1 2 Вұ в„Ұ k 4ПҖT - i(Оұ + ОІ)О»k F 4ПҖT + ПҲ (1) 1 2 Вұ в„Ұ k 4ПҖT + i(Оұ + ОІ)О»k F 4ПҖT -ПҲ (1) 1 2 Вұ в„Ұ k 4ПҖT - i(Оұ + ОІ)О»k F 4ПҖT =0,
div.p: where the upper (lower) sign in the symbol Вұ is for i = 1 (i = 3). With this result and the fact that Eq. ( 32) vanishes when в„Ұ k = Пү ОҪ = 0, B s2 (0, 0) = 0. Thus, O(Пү) terms in Eq. ( 31) originate only from the terms containing the derivative of B s1,2,3 , i.e.

When Пғ SJ(normal) xy Пғ SS(normal) xy , either anomalous MT terms or the sum of the DOS term and the regular part of the MT terms is dominant, depending on the magnitude of Оі П• . In the clean limit, the DOS terms with side jump process is dominant when Пғ SJ(normal) xy Пғ SS(normal) xy . VII. ACKNOWLEDGEMENTSThis work was supported by JSPS KAKENHI Grant Number 19K05253 and 20K20891. AW and YK thank Yuta Suzuki for his comments on the intrinsic spin Hall effect in the Rashba model. APPENDIX: LIST OF SYMBOLS T : temperature, which has the dimension of energy in the present paper because we set k B = 1. ОІ: 1/T inverse temperature. T c : transition temperature.

figure.head: j
figure.figDesc: s : spin current density operator or the uniform component of Fourier transform of the spin current density operator. j c : charge current density operator or the uniform component of Fourier transform of the charge current density operator.

figure.head: N

figure.head: Figure 4
figure.figDesc: Figure 4 shows the diagrams for the AL terms with the skew scattering process. Among them, only the diagram shown in Fig. 4(d) has Пғ z -Оұ-Оұ and hence it has the opposite sign to other diagrams (a), (b), and (c). The diagram reflected with respect to the horizontal axis (i.e., the diagram "upside-down") contributes the spin Hall conductivity equally to the original diagram. Thus the contributions of (a), (b), (c), and (d) should be doubled. The response function can be written in the form of

Thus k 1x k 2y (k 1x k 2yk 1y k 2x ) in the original diagram corresponds to k 2x k 1y (k 2x k 1yk 2y k 1x ) in the reflected diagram.
